feature.title: Dispatch Batch Builder and Operator History
feature.purpose: Upgrade the Atlas Dispatch Console from single staged drafts into grouped command batches with a browser-local operator history and copy recovery path.

research.name: Bulk Action UX
research.takeaway: Bulk actions should save time while preserving trust, clear target counts, and mobile-friendly controls.

research.name: Transactional Outbox Pattern
research.takeaway: Outbox flows should stage messages durably before a separate sender processes them; Atlas keeps this local-only and does not send.

research.name: Mission Control Visibility
research.takeaway: Agent command centers need recent activity and task state visibility so operators can understand what agents are doing.

research.name: Minimal Command Center Architecture
research.takeaway: A lightweight static command center remains easy for agents and users to inspect, modify, and run without a heavy build stack.

interactions: Stage top three visible suggestions into a grouped local batch.
Stage all visible suggestions into the local outbox.
Group staged drafts by dispatch kind with source-lane chips.
Open source lane Comms from grouped batch chips.
Copy all staged commands as one batch.
Show a manual copy buffer when browser clipboard access is unavailable.
Archive all staged drafts and clear local operator history.
implementation.safetyBoundary: The feature only stages, copies, groups, archives, and logs local drafts. It does not send messages, approve gates, mutate the control plane, or start workers.

snapshot.topSuggestion: Review gate before any live action
